# Pairee
> A modern, dual-pane, modular terminal file manager (TUI) written in Rust with Ratatui and Tokio.

Pairee combines the classic visual convenience of double-panel navigation (inspired by Norton Commander) with modern asynchronous operations, customizable keyboard shortcuts, dynamic themes, multi-language support, and a smart self-updater.

## Key Resources
- [Pairee Official Website](https://pairee.fitty.ar): Landing page and presentation.
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/FittyAr/Pairee): Main source code repository.
- [Latest Releases](https://github.com/FittyAr/Pairee/releases): Release logs and precompiled binaries.

## Licensing
- **Pairee Core Project**: Licensed under **GNU GPL v3**.
- **Website presentation files**: Licensed under **MIT License**.

## Features
- **Dual-Pane Interface**: Parallel navigation to copy/move/delete without losing context.
- **Asynchronous Engine**: Non-blocking operations powered by Tokio runtime.
- **Customizable Control Profiles**: Includes built-in configurations for Norton Classic, Vim, and modern controls.
- **Dynamic Styling**: Customizable Slate, Blue, and other themes.
- **Self-Updater**: Supports up to 13 installation channels, verifying packages via SHA-256 signatures.
- **Extra Tools**: Directory difference viewer, system process manager, advanced text/file finder, and detailed attribute panel.

## Quick Installation

### Linux / macOS
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/FittyAr/Pairee/master/scripts/install.sh | bash
```

### Windows (PowerShell)
```powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/FittyAr/Pairee/master/scripts/install.ps1 | iex
```
